title: Recycle bale comprising cellulose ester
abstract: A bale of sheets made of virgin and/or waste/recycle cellulose fibers and cellulose ester (CE) staple fibers can be fed to a hydropulper. The CE staple fibers have: i. a denier per filament (DPF) of less than 3, or ii. a cut length of less than 6 mm, or iii. crimping, or iv. a combination of any two or more of (i)-(iii). The bales can be fed and dropped in as entire bales to a hydropulper, or a blend tank directly or indirectly in fluid communication with a hydropulper, using the same feed system employed for feeding cellulose to the hydopulper and avoiding having to defiberize by pulling or drawing fibers from a box container into a hydropulper.
claims:
Claims What We Claim Is: 1. A bale comprising sheets, wherein at least one of the sheets in the bale comprises waste/recycle cellulose fibers and cellulose ester (CE) staple fibers, wherein said CE staple fibers have: i. a denier per filament (DPF) of less than 3, or ii. a cut length of less than 6 mm, or iii. crimping, or iv. a combination of any two or more of (i)-(iii). 2. A bale comprising sheets, wherein at least one of the sheets in the bale comprises virgin cellulose fibers and cellulose ester (CE) staple fibers, wherein said CE staple fibers have: i. a denier per filament (DPF) of less than 3, or ii. a cut length of less than 6 mm, or iii. crimping, or iv. a combination of any two or more of (i)-(iii). 3. The bale of claim 1 , wherein the waste/recycle cellulose fibers are deinked. 4. The bale of any one of claims 1 or 3, wherein the bale is obtained from a recycle facility. 5. The bale of any one of claims 1 or 3-4, wherein the waste/recycle cellulose fibers are obtained from OCC, ONP, Office Waste, Mixed paper, or High Grade De-lnked Paper. 6. The bale of any one of claims 1 -5, wherein the sheets within the bale are compressed. 7. The bale of any one of claims 1 -6, wherein each sheet is at least 1 mm thick. 8. The bale of any one of claims 1 -7, having dimensions of width x length x height of 12ââx12ââx 12ââ and up to 42â x 42â x 30â. 9. The bale of any one of claims 1 -8, each sheet in the bale has the same width and length as the bale, and the bale height is comprised of the height of the stacked sheets, discounting packaging and strapping. 10. The bale of any one of claims 1 -9, wherein the bales are flat on all sides. 1 1. The bale of any one of claims 1 -10, wherein one or more of the sheets is a slab having a thickness of at least 2 inches. 12. The bale of any one of claims 1 -11 , wherein the bale has straps and/or packaging made with or obtained from a cellulose pulp. 13. The bale of any one of claims 1 -12, wherein the CE staple fibers have a target cut length of less than 6 mm, and a ratio of cut length to DPF not more than 5:1. 14. The bale of any one of claims 1 -13, wherein the waste/recyle cellulose fibera and CE staple fibers are co-refined. 15. The bale of any one of claims 1 -14, wherein the CE staple fibers have a DPF of less than 3.0, a cut length of less than 6 mm, are non-round, and are crimped. 16. The bale of any one of claims 1 -15, wherein at least 90% of the fibers are randomly oriented, and the sheets within the bale are compressed. 17. The bale of any one of claims 1 -16, wherein each sheet has a thickness of not more than 60 mm or greater than 2 inches. 18. The bale of any one of claims 1 -17, wherein the moisture of a sheet in the bale is from 1 to 25 wt.%, based on oven dryness. 19. The bale of any one of claims 1 -18, wherein one or more of the sheets is a slab having a thickness of at least 2 inches, and the slabs are flash dried. 20. The bale of any one of claims 1 -19, wherein not more than 1 wt.% of a surface hydrolyzing binder is added to the CE staple fibers, or the sheets do not contain more than 1 wt.% of a surface hydrolyzing binder, or no surface hydrolyzing binder is added to or contained in the sheets. 21. The bales of any one of claim 1 -20, wherein the CE staple fibers are crimped. 22. The bale of any one of claims 1 -21 , wherein at least 99 wt.% of all fibers in the Composition are cellulose fibers and CE staple fibers. 23. A process for feeding bales to a hydropulper, comprising feeding a CE containing bale to a hydropulper, or a blend tank directly or indirectly in fluid 

 communication with a hydropulper, wherein at least one of said sheets comprises waste/recycle cellulose fibers and cellulose ester (CE) staple fibers, said CE staple fibers having: i. a denier per filament (DPF) of less than 3, or ii. a cut length of less than 6 mm, or iii. crimping, or iv. a combination of any two or more of (i)-(iii). 24. A process for feeding bales to a hydropulper, comprising feeing a CE containing bale to a hydropulper, or a blend tank directly or indirectly in fluid communication with a hydropulper, wherein at least one of said sheets comprises virgin cellulose fibers and cellulose ester (CE) staple fibers, said CE staple fibers having: i. a denier per filament (DPF) of less than 3, or ii. a cut length of less than 6 mm, or iii. crimping, or iv. a combination of any two or more of (i)-(iii). 25. The process of any one of claims 23-24, wherein bales that do not contain CE staple fibers are fed to the hydropulper with a feed system, and the CE containing bales are fed to the hydropulper on the same feed system. 26. The process of any one of claims 23-25, wherein bale has straps and/or packaging, and the bale is fed with the straps, or the packaging, or both to the hydropulper. 27. The process of any one of claims 23-26, wherein the bale is delivered to a wet laid facility, and the bales are not processed to contain their shape after unstrapping and unwrapping the bale. 28. The process of any one of claims 23-27, wherein the bale is not defiberized prior to feeding the bale to a hydropulper. 29. The process of any one of claims 23-28, wherein the bale, after optionally unstrapping and unwrapping, is deposited as a bale without modification to a hydropulper. 30. Use of a bale of sheets, in which at least one of the sheets contains cellulose fibers that are virgin, waste/recycle, or both, and cellulose ester (CE) 

 staple fibers, to feed a hydropulper or a blend tank directly in indirectly in fluid communication with a hydropulper for making wet laid products, wherein the CE staple fibers have v. a denier per filament (DPF) of less than 3, or vi. a cut length of less than 6 mm, or vii. crimping, or viii. a combination of any two or more of (i)-(iii).
